description_long: This hostel is a long, multi-sectioned building with a combination of architectural styles, featuring a prominent L-shaped layout. The facade displays a series of arched colonnades on the ground floor, with rectangular windows above, and a mix of roof types including red-tiled and flat sections. The rightmost section is more ornate, with detailed stonework and a slightly protruding corner, while the central and left sections are simpler, with repetitive window patterns and lighter-colored walls. The building appears to be constructed primarily from masonry and concrete, with visible window frames and balcony railings. The overall structure suggests a historic or classical influence, possibly Mediterranean or Colonial, with decorative elements and a focus on symmetry. The hostel is likely to accommodate a large number of guests, with multiple entry points and communal spaces.
description: A long, L-shaped hostel building with arched colonnades, mixed roof styles, and ornate stonework on one end. The facade features repetitive windows and classical architectural details.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full length of the hostel, highlighting the arched colonnades, repetitive windows, and the ornate rightmost section.
description_view_1: The right side view emphasizes the detailed stonework and window arrangement of the prominent corner section.
description_view_2: The top-down view reveals the L-shaped layout, roof variations, and the overall footprint of the building.
description_view_3: The left side view shows the simpler end of the building, with visible roof tiles and a less ornate facade.
attribute_entrance_design: The main entrance is located along the front facade, beneath the arched colonnade. It features large doorways set within the arches, likely made of wood or metal, and is accessible via a flat approach with no visible steps or ramps. The entrance is integrated into the architectural rhythm of the arches, with no prominent canopies or decorative overhangs.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a repetitive, symmetrical pattern across all facades, with rectangular shapes above the arches and more ornate, arched windows in the rightmost section. The windows are evenly spaced, with some sections featuring double rows and others single rows, framed by subtle decorative elements.
attribute_balcony_presence: Balconies are present along the front facade, particularly above the arched colonnade. They are small, with metal railings and are evenly distributed, providing outdoor access for several rooms.
attribute_architectural_style: The building exhibits a blend of Colonial and Mediterranean architectural styles, characterized by arched colonnades, red-tiled roofs, and detailed stonework on the corner section.
